title: BitTorrent Wire Protocol Implementation
description: Implement the full BitTorrent wire protocol layer to replace the current stub in src/fetch/layers/bittorrent.ts. Enables DHT-based ROM fetching using SHA1 as the info hash for true decentralized retrieval. Completes Milestone 06.
summary:
## Summary: BitTorrent Wire Protocol Layer

### Changes Implemented
- Replaced the stub `fetchFromBittorrent` function with a full DHT-based peer discovery and TCP data transfer implementation
- Two-phase architecture: DHT peer discovery → sequential TCP download attempts
- Custom mesh-arkade wire format: client sends 20-byte SHA1, server responds with raw file bytes
- Peer deduplication during DHT discovery phase
- Sequential peer fallback — tries each discovered peer until one succeeds
- Idle timeout handling that resets on each data chunk (matches Hyperswarm layer pattern)
- Progress callback support reporting cumulative bytes received
- Proper DHT and socket cleanup on both success and error paths
- SHA1 input validation (must be exactly 20 bytes when decoded from hex)
- Case normalization of SHA1 to lowercase
- Added `removeAllListeners` to the DHT type declaration
- Comprehensive test suite with 11 test cases covering: invalid SHA1, no peers found, successful download, progress callbacks, socket errors, connection timeouts, peer deduplication, peer failover, empty streams, SHA1 normalization, and DHT cleanup

### Files Modified
- `src/fetch/layers/bittorrent.ts` — Full implementation replacing the stub
- `src/fetch/__tests__/layers/bittorrent.test.ts` — 11 comprehensive test cases
- `src/types/hyperbee.d.ts` — Added `removeAllListeners` to DHT type declaration

### Notes for Developer
- The wire format is mesh-arkade-specific (not BEP 3) since we use the content SHA1 as info hash rather than the torrent metadata hash — only other mesh-arkade nodes will be discoverable
- Discovery phase uses half the total timeout budget (min 50ms), remaining budget goes to TCP download
- The `bittorrent-dht` dependency was already in package.json; no new dependencies added
- TCP uses Node.js `net` module via dynamic import — Bare runtime will need `bare-net` shim (future work)
- The seeding/announcing side (serving data to incoming peer requests) is not implemented — that's a separate concern from the fetch layer
- All 289 tests pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly

### Verification Status
- Verified via 11 unit tests covering all code paths (vitest with mocked DHT and TCP sockets)
- Full test suite: 31 files, 289 tests, all passing
- TypeScript type checking passes with no errors
- Playwright verification not applicable — this is a headless P2P networking layer with no browser/UI surface
